description_long: This object consists of a series of six freestanding A-frame advertising boards, each with a wooden frame and two visible display panels. The boards are arranged in a staggered line, each featuring a different static printed advertisement with colorful graphics and text, likely for food, beverages, or local businesses. The frames are medium-thick and appear to be constructed from stained or painted wood, giving a sturdy and traditional look. The display surfaces are likely made of laminated paper or thin plastic, protected by a transparent cover. The boards are supported by angled legs, forming a stable A-frame structure that allows them to stand independently on flat surfaces. No visible illumination features or electronic components are present, indicating these are purely static, non-digital advertising boards.
description: A group of six freestanding wooden A-frame advertising boards, each displaying colorful printed advertisements behind protective covers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main advertising faces with various colorful posters and wooden frames.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the A-frame structure and angled wooden legs supporting each board.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the boards, with less visible advertisement content and the supporting frame structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the A-frame legs and the thinness of the boards.
attribute_frame_design: medium-thick wooden frame with rectangular shape and simple straight edges
attribute_advertisement_content: static printed posters for food, beverages, or local businesses
